content: Ovarian cancer is the most lethal gynecologic malignancy, often presenting at advanced stage due to vague symptoms. Risk factors: BRCA1/2 mutations (lifetime risk 40-60% for BRCA1, 15-30% for BRCA2), Lynch syndrome, family history, nulliparity, endometriosis (for clear cell and endometrioid subtypes). Protective factors: oral contraceptives, tubal ligation, salpingectomy, breastfeeding. Histologic subtypes: high-grade serous (70%, TP53 mutations nearly universal, BRCA1/2 mutations 15-25%); low-grade serous (KRAS/BRAF mutations); endometrioid (often associated with endometriosis); clear cell (endometriosis association, chemotherapy resistant); mucinous (often metastatic from GI tract); borderline tumors (low malignant potential). Many 'ovarian' cancers originate in fallopian tube fimbriae. CA-125 elevated in 80% of epithelial ovarian cancers but not specific (elevated in endometriosis, fibroids, menstruation, other cancers).
